name: Zinc in Water
description: Zinc concentrations (mg/L) in bottom vs surface water at 10 paired locations.
studyDescription: Researchers measured zinc concentrations in water samples collected from the bottom and surface at 10 locations along a stream. Each location provides a natural pairing — the same site measured at two depths. This is an observational study comparing zinc levels by depth.

variableDescriptions.bottom: Zinc concentration (mg/L) measured in water collected from the bottom of the stream at each location.
variableDescriptions.surface: Zinc concentration (mg/L) measured in water collected from the surface of the stream at the same location.

sourceDetail: Gibbons, R.D. (1994). Statistical Methods for Groundwater Monitoring. Wiley. Also used in Penn State STAT 500 and many introductory statistics textbooks.

context.parameter: mean difference in zinc concentration (bottom − surface)
context.nullClaim: no difference in zinc concentration between bottom and surface water
